summary:
Specialized phonological awareness training is recommended only for preschool children who show signs of reading difficulty or delay; it is not necessary for typically developing children. Therefore, such training should not be provided universally to every preschooler.\boxed{\text{No}}

The summary focuses solely on clinically significant findings related to when targeted interventions like phonological awareness training are appropriate — emphasizing risk factors rather than universal application across all young children.
